title: Drive External Sharing Alerts
spl:
index=gws sourcetype="gws:drive" event_name="change_user_access"
| where new_value="people_with_link" OR NOT match(target_user_email, ".*@yourdomain\\.com")
| table _time, actor.email, doc_title, target_user_email, new_value
| sort -_time
description: Alerts on files and folders shared outside the primary domain or with `anyone with link`‚Äîcomplements anomaly baselines (UC-11.2.3).
value: Alerts on files and folders shared outside the primary domain or with `anyone with link`‚Äîcomplements anomaly baselines (UC-11.2.3).
implementation: Tune domain pattern via lookup. Alert on shares to competitor domains or public links on confidential folders (path regex).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Drive audit log (`change_user_access`, `shared_drive_settings_change`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Tune domain pattern via lookup. Alert on shares to competitor domains or public links on confidential folders (path regex).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=gws sourcetype="gws:drive" event_name="change_user_access"
| where new_value="people_with_link" OR NOT match(target_user_email, ".*@yourdomain\\.com")
| table _time, actor.email, doc_title, target_user_email, new_value
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Drive External Sharing Alerts** ‚Äî Alerts on files and folders shared outside the primary domain or with `anyone with link`‚Äîcomplements anomaly baselines (UC-11.2.3).

Documented **Data sources**: Drive audit log (`change_user_access`, `shared_drive_settings_change`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: gws; **sourcetype**: gws:drive. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=gws, sourcetype="gws:drive". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where new_value="people_with_link" OR NOT match(target_user_email, ".*@yourdomain\\.com")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Drive External Sharing Alerts**): table _time, actor.email, doc_title, target_user_email, new_value
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with Google Admin console audit and Google Workspace reports for the same org unit and time window.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (external shares), Bar chart (domains), Timeline.
grandmaExplanation: We watch unusual file sharing, so a burst of outside links is not the first time we learn about a leak.
knownFalsePositives: Customer extranet folders, partner portals, and shared research drives with expected external access.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
